title: Studio Architecture Evolution
organization: Two Marshalls Studios — Studio Operating System (TMS-OS)
summary: Preserves the verified architectural evolution from the portal-centered foundation to Fully Modular StudioDB.

sections.title: Purpose
paragraphs: This document preserves the verified evolution of the Two Marshalls Studios architecture from an early portal-centered structure to the current Fully Modular StudioDB operating model.
Its purpose is to explain how the architecture changed, why major structural changes mattered, and how the current system was reached without inventing unsupported dates or development stages.

sections.title: Historical Control Rule
paragraphs: Architecture evolution must be recorded from verified completed work and recovered evidence, not from assumptions about what may have happened.
Where an exact calendar date is not verified, the development sequence is preserved without inventing a date. Future architecture ideas remain in ENH-LOG-001 until approved, completed, and verified.

sections.title: Starting Architecture — Portal-Centered Foundation
paragraphs: The early verified architecture centered on establishing the Studio Portal as the visible command center for Two Marshalls Studios.
items: Studio information was presented through the portal.
Individual pages and local files carried more responsibility than they do in the current architecture.
The studio needed a stronger shared data foundation so important information would not remain scattered across separate pages or workstations.
The long-term operating requirement was for studio work to remain accessible from multiple locations and computers.

sections.title: Evolution to a Central Studio Database
paragraphs: The architecture evolved by establishing StudioDB as the structured data foundation of TMS-OS.
This changed the architecture from primarily page-centered information handling toward a governed structured record system.

sections.title: Documentation Integration
paragraphs: The next verified architectural change connected documentation to the shared StudioDB structure.
This reduced the need for separate page-specific information sources and moved the studio toward a shared operating model.

sections.title: Data Consolidation
paragraphs: This stage reduced fragmentation, improved consistency, and prepared the structured data for broader shared use.

sections.title: Evolution of Search
paragraphs: Search evolved from a local page function into a shared studio capability across supported departments.

sections.title: Transition to Modular Studio Data
paragraphs: As the structured system grew, the architecture evolved away from relying on one large permanent studio data file.
These modular sources were combined into a shared studio data object for portal use.

sections.title: MILE-013 — Fully Modular StudioDB
paragraphs: MILE-013 - Fully Modular StudioDB is the last verified completed development milestone.
This milestone established the current Fully Modular StudioDB architecture.

sections.title: Evolution of the Source-of-Truth Model
items: TMS-OS and StudioDB govern structured studio records.
The Studio Portal renders and exposes governed information.
GitHub preserves source code and project history.
Google Headquarters preserves permanent human-readable documentation.
Temporary chat, screenshots, local files, and uploads may provide evidence but must not become competing permanent sources of truth.

sections.title: Establishment of Google Headquarters
paragraphs: During historical recovery, the permanent Google Headquarters structure was created and verified.
This added a permanent human-readable documentation layer to the studio architecture.

sections.title: Evolution Through Historical Recovery
paragraphs: After MILE-013, the studio entered MILE-014 - Complete Historical Recovery.
This period strengthened the architecture by recovering important knowledge from temporary or fragmented sources and converting verified information into permanent governed records.

sections.title: Permanent Procedures Added to the Architecture
items: PROC-001 established the controlled Work Session Procedure.
PROC-002 established the controlled Bug Fix Procedure.
PROC-003 established the Documentation Update Procedure.
ENH-LOG-001 established permanent enhancement tracking from the beginning of the project.
DOC-INDEX-001 established permanent document indexing and verification.
HIST-001 established the verified studio historical timeline.
ARCH-001 established the verified current architecture.

sections.title: Major Architectural Shifts
items: From portal-centered information toward structured StudioDB records.
From isolated page data toward shared governed data.
From a large central data structure toward modular studio-data files.
From local search toward cross-department shared search.
From workstation dependence toward shared source control and permanent cloud documentation.
From temporary knowledge preservation toward controlled permanent records.
From enhancement ideas interrupting active work toward deferred controlled enhancement tracking.

sections.title: Current Architecture Result
paragraphs: The verified result of this evolution is the Fully Modular StudioDB operating model.
items: Modular studio-data files — separated data responsibilities
Shared studio data object — unified portal data access
GitHub — source code and project history
Enhancement Ideas Log — controlled deferred improvement tracking

sections.title: Current Evolution Checkpoint
items: Last completed development milestone: MILE-013 - Fully Modular StudioDB
Current active milestone: MILE-014 - Complete Historical Recovery

sections.title: Architecture Change Control Going Forward
paragraphs: Future architecture changes must not be mixed into unrelated work sessions or bug fixes unless Stephen explicitly approves a separate path.

A proposed architecture improvement must first be captured in ENH-LOG-001 when it is not part of approved current work. Once approved and completed, the change must be reflected in the appropriate permanent records.

sections.title: Relationship to Other Permanent Records
items: ARCH-001 describes the architecture that exists now.
ARCH-EVO-001 explains how the verified architecture changed over time.
HIST-001 preserves the broader studio historical sequence.
STATE-001 records the current system checkpoint.
ROADMAP-001 separates completed, active, and future direction.
ENH-LOG-001 preserves future improvement ideas that are not yet verified history.

sections.title: Permanent Statement
paragraphs: Two Marshalls Studios evolved from a portal-centered foundation into a Fully Modular StudioDB operating model through verified stages of database creation, documentation integration, data consolidation, shared search, modularization, source control, and permanent documentation governance.
This record must continue to preserve actual architectural change without confusing future ideas with completed evolution.

revisionHistory.summary: Connected the verified permanent document to the shared structured Document Template Engine.

verification.method: Converted from the recovered DOCX source used to create the verified Google Headquarters document.
